revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Dairizuka Kofun]][[ja:内裏塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Ancient Kofun in Futtsu, Kantõ, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Dairizuka kofun | native_name =内裏塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Dairizuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Dairizuka kofun | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Dairizuka kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.325944|N|139.857167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Futamazuka|qid=Q96155764|1=Futamazuka}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11394584|1=Dairizuka Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Dairizuka kofun'''|内裏塚古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kashima neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Futtsu, Chiba|Futtsu]], {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002, with the area under protection extended in 2009.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211990|title=内裏塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Dairizuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in an estuary lowland created by the {{ill|Koito River|en|Koito River|qid=Q6426373|lt=Koito River|1=Koito River}}, which flows into {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|qid=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay|1=Tokyo Bay}}, in the central part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}. The area along this river has a high concentration of Kofun period remains, including 47 tumuli (29 dome-shaped, seven square-shaped and 11 keyhole-shaped), which were built from the middleof the 5th century to the 7th century AD. This area was a transportation hub, controlling the route from the Bōsō Peninsula to the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|qid=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}} and from thence to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} and the Yamato court. The Dairizuka Kofun was built in two layers with an overall length of 144 meters, and was originally surrounded by a moat. The mound was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and was surrounded by three rows cylindrical, "morning-glory"-shaped, house-shaped, and human-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1906 by [[:en:Tokyo Imperial University|Tokyo Imperial University]], and two stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s aligned with the main axis of the tumulus were found in the posterior circular portion the mound. The eastern chamber measured 5.75 meters long by 0.75 to 0.88 meters in width, and contained two sets of human remains, five iron swords, one iron dagger, one iron sickle, three chopsticks, an iron glove and ax. The western chamber was 7.55 meters long by one meter wide, and had a [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]], five iron swords, one long iron sword, one iron spear, a set of gold-bronze metal fittings and a ''{{ill|Kabura-ya|en|Kabura-ya|qid=Q6344481|lt=Kabura-ya|1=Kabura-ya}}'' arrowhead made of deer bone. From the [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s it was determined that this tomb was built in the middleof the Kofun period, or early 5th century AD. The metal fittings are significant, as an identical set has been recovered from a tomb in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} and the only other known location has been from tombs in the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Mimana region]] of the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|qid=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}. The ''Kabura-ya'' arrowhead, which is used to make a noise when the arrow is launched, is a unique find. These artifacts are on display at the {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|en|National Museum of Japanese History|qid=Q3330690|lt=National Museum of Japanese History|1=National Museum of Japanese History}} in {{ill|Sakura, Chiba|en|Sakura, Chiba|qid=Q498011|lt=Sakura, Chiba|1=Sakura, Chiba}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The area where this ''kofun'' is located is ancient Kingdom of Sue (須恵), which was also on the route the semi-mythical {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} took on his expedition to conquer eastern Japan for the Yamato court. Per the ''[[Kujiki]]'' chronicle, Sue was ruled by a ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named {{nihongo|Ōfuhiomi no Mikoto|息大布日意弥命|||qq=}}, and as this is the largest keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' in the southern Kantō region, it is possible that it is the grave of that local ruler.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Per local legend, it is the grave of a daughter of {{ill|Takahashi no Mushimaro|en|Takahashi no Mushimaro|qid=Q840963|lt=Takahashi no Mushimaro|1=Takahashi no Mushimaro}}, a court official whose poems appear in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}''; however, he lived in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, at least two centuries after this tumulus was completed. The tumulus is a ten minute walk from {{ill|Aohori Station|en|Aohori Station|qid=Q558935|lt=Aohori Station|1=Aohori Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uchibō Line|en|Uchibō Line|qid=Q1142964|lt=Uchibō Line|1=Uchibō Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Total length: 144 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 88 meters wide x 13 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 88 meter diameter x 13 meters high, 2-tiers ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|qid=Q6573257|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{commons Category:dd]]{{template-inline|Dairizuka-kofun|qq=}} *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-034.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.futtsu.lg.jp/0000000782.html Futtsu City official home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Dairizuka Kofun]]
<!-- {{URL|example.com|qq=}} --> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Dairizuka-kofun}} [[ja:内裏塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394583}} [[Category:Futtsu]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
